pick.rationale: Illinois holds a decisive advantage in advanced metrics, ranking 6th–7th nationally with the top-ranked offense in the country and a top-25 defense, while North Carolina ranks 27th–29th with solid but significantly less elite efficiency on both ends. The Tar Heels are also still without star freshman Caleb Wilson due to a season-ending thumb injury, a loss that has weakened their frontcourt depth and scoring versatility. Although UNC showed resilience in their Round of 64 win over VCU, Illinois’s overwhelming offensive firepower and superior overall profile make them the clear favorite in this Round of 32 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois vs North Carolina Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois is heavily favored due to elite offensive metrics, strong overall ratings, and North Carolina's ongoing absence of key player Caleb Wilson.
evidence: Illinois ranks 6th (Torvik) and 7th (KenPom) nationally; North Carolina ranks 27th and 29th.
Illinois has the #1 offense nationally per KenPom and #2 per Torvik; UNC's offense ranks 32nd–33rd.
Prior pick confirms Caleb Wilson is out for the season with a broken thumb, weakening UNC's roster.
Illinois defeated Penn 96-52 in Round of 64, showcasing dominant form; UNC narrowly beat VCU despite missing Wilson.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
